# Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Data Connector - Full Documentation

# Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Data Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This example shows how to execute a search for assignments and get the search results.

This image shows the assignment search example flow:

![Search assignment flow with HTTP Listener, Assignment Search operation, and Error handling](_images/data-connector-assignments-search-flow.jpg)

## Configure the HTTP Listener Source

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the source for the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.
3.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
4.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
5.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
6.  Specify `/assignmentsSearch` for the **Path** field.

    ![HTTP Listener General configuration example values](_images/data-connector-http-listener-config.jpg)

7.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP listener in the app.
    1.  On the **General** tab, enter `8081` in **Port**.
    2.  Click **Test Connection**.
    3.  Click **OK**.

8.  Save the Mule project. .

## Configure the Assignments Search Operation

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select the **Assignment Search** operation from the palette and
    drag it on to the flow.
2.  Navigate to `http://localhost:8081/assignmentsSearch`, open Postman, and copy the required body
    for the **Assignments Search** operation:

    ![Assignments Search operation example Content body](_images/data-connector-assignment-search-body.jpg)

    A sample **Assignments Search** operation payload is shown here:

```json
{
  "count": 4,
  "query": {
    "textQuery": {
      "fields": ["description"],
      "searchPhrase": "*"
    }
  },
  "start": 2
}
```

1.  Add the body you obtained in the previous step to the **Content** parameter.
2.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure B2C Data
    Connector with the required values:

    ![OAuth 2 Application Connection Provider authentication configuration example](_images/data-api-connector-oauth2-configuration.jpg)

3.  Click **Test Connection**.
4.  Save the Mule project.

## Run the Mule Application

Run the project as a Mule application:

1.  In **Package Explorer**, right-click the project name and select **Run As > Mule Application**.
2.  Navigate to `http://localhost:8081/assignmentsSearch`, open Postman, and check the response.

You see the search data result in the output.

### XML Example

Paste this code into the **Configuration XML** tab in Studio to quickly load the flow for this
example into your Mule app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:commerce-cloud-dataapi="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-dataapi" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-dataapi http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-dataapi/current/mule-commerce-cloud-dataapi.xsd">
	<flow name="assignments-searchFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="8081/pricing/assignments/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/assignments"  config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/pricing/assignments/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/assignments"/>
		<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-pricing-assignments-organizations-assignments-by-version-organization-id doc:name="Assignments Search"
    config-ref="Commerce_Cloud_Data_Connector_Config" version="#[attributes.uriParams.version]" organizationId="#[attributes.uriParams.organizationId]" siteId="#[attributes.queryParams.siteId]">
			<commerce-cloud-dataapi:content ><![CDATA[#[{
  "count": 4,
  "query": {
    "textQuery": {
      "fields": [
        "description"
      ],
      "searchPhrase": "*"
    }
  },
  "start": 2
}]]]></commerce-cloud-dataapi:content>
		</commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-pricing-assignments-organizations-assignments-by-version-organization-id>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

After adding the code in the Configuration XML tab, you can see the flow in the \*Message Flow\* tab
of the canvas.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [B2C Data Connector Reference](commerce-cloud-b2c-data-connector-reference)
---

# Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Data Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Data (B2C Data Connector) is an operation-based
connector, which means that when you add the connector to your flow, you need to configure a
specific operation for the connector to perform.

B2C Data Connector supports OAuth 2 authentication configuration for connections.

## Limitations

- Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Data Connector and Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector
  cannot be run in the same application.

## OAuth2 Configuration

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#DataConfiguration_Oauth2Application">Oauth2 Application Connection Provider</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Oauth2 Application Connection Provider

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>false</code>, each connection is closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections to keep open concurrently.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the connection Idle Timeout.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to stream received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space, in bytes, for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Uri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own base URI.</p></td><td><p>default base URI</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are HTTP and HTTPS.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, the deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider. Contact Salesforce for this information.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider. Contact Salesforce for this information.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p>default token URL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Assign Product To Category

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:assign-product-to-category>`

Creates a category product assignment using the information provided. This operation makes an HTTP
PUT request to the `/product/catalogs/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/products/{productId}`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for a request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors..</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Assign Variation For Master Product

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:assign-variation-for-master-product>`

Assign a variation for a product master. If the variation product does not initially exist, one is
created using the body that is passed in. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/product/catalogs/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/{masterProductId}/variations` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>masterProductId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Master product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Assign Variation Group To Master Product

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:assign-variation-group-to-master-product>`

Assign a variation group for a product master. If the variation group product does not initially
exist, one is created using the body that is passed in. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to
the `/product/catalogs/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/{masterProductId}/variation-groups`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>masterProductId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Master product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors..</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Assignments Search

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:assignment-search>`

Performs a search operation for Assignments. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
`/pricing/assignments/{{version}}/organizations/{{organizationId}}/assignments` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default ## Campaigns Search

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:campaigns-search>`

Searches for campaigns using a search request object that allows filtering on various attributes.
This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/pricing/campaigns/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/campaigns endpoint.

### Parameters

allows filtering on various attributes. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
`/pricing/coupons/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/coupons/redemptions` endpoint.

- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Campaign

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-campaign>`

Creates a campaign using the information provided. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/pricing/campaigns/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/campaigns/{campaignId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>campaignId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the campaign</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout..</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Coupon

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-coupon>`

Creates a coupon using the information provided. If a coupon exists with the same unique identifier,
the coupon is replaced completely. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/pricing/coupons/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/coupons/{couponId}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>couponId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the coupon to create.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Customer In Customer List

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-customer-in-customer-list>`

Action to create a new customer. The customer is created using the specified customer number,
credentials, and customer information.

This action verifies the following:

- Login acceptance criteria and uniqueness
- Mandatory customer properties

If the action fails to create the customer, it returns a `400` error with an appropriate message.

This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/customer/customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customer-lists/{listId}/customers/{customerNo}`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>listId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List ID to which to add the customer.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>customerNo</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Customer number.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Customer List

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-customer-list>`

Action to create a new customer. The customer is created using the specified credentials and
customer information.

This action verifies the following:

- Login acceptance criteria and uniqueness
- Mandatory customer properties

If the action fails to create the customer, it returns a `400` error with an appropriate message.

This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/customer/customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customer-lists/{listId}/customers`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default ## Create Gift Certificate

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-gift-certificate>`

Creates a gift certificate using the information provided. If a gift certificate with the same
unique identifier is generated, it is deleted and a new one is created. This operation makes an HTTP
PUT request to the
`/pricing/gift-certificates/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/gift-certificates` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Product

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-product>`

Creates a product using the information provided. If a product with the same unique identifier
exists, it is overwritten unless the header `x-dw-validate-existing=true` is passed in with the
request.

This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/pricing/gift-certificates/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/gift-certificates` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use..</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID..</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Product Option

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-product-option>`

Creates a local product option using the information provided or binds a shared product option to
the product.

This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/product/products/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/products/{productId}/product-options/{id}`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the local or shared product option.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create promotion

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-promotion>`

Creates a promotion using the information provided. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
`/pricing/promotions/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/promotions/{id}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Variation Attribute Value

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:create-variation-attribute-value>`

Action to create a product variation attribute value. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to
the
`product/products/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/products/{productId}/variation-attributes/{attributeId}/values/{id}`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the product.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the variation attribute value.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>attributeId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Variation attribute ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Campaign

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:delete-campaign>`

Deletes the campaign by ID. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
`/pricing/campaigns/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/campaigns/{campaignId}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default campaign.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>siteId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID used as a query parameter to query the data for a specific site.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
`/customer/customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customer-lists/{listId}/customers/{customerNo}`

the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Product

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:delete-product>`

Deletes the product by ID. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
`/product/products/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/products/{productId}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Product Option

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:delete-product-option>`

Deletes a local product option or unbinds a shared product option from the product. This operation
makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/product/products/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/products/{productId}/product-options/{id}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Product ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the local or shared product option</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Variation Attribute Value

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:delete-variation-attribute-value>`

Deletes the variation attribute value of a variation attribute bound to a master product. This
operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/product/products/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/products/{productId}/variation-attributes/{attributeId}/values/{id}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the product</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>attributeId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The variation attribute ID</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the variation attribute value</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Address For Customer From Customer List

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:get-address-for-customer-from-customer-list>`

Action that returns a single customer address from a customer list. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the
`/customer/customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customer-lists/{listId}/customers/{customerNo}/addresses/{addressId}`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>customerNo</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Customer number.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>listId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List Id for the list from which to return the customer.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Integer</p></div></div></td><td><p>The query parameter. Maximum number of records to retrieve per request. The minimum value is <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Integer</p></div></div></td><td><p>The query parameter. Used to retrieve the results based on a particular resource offset. The maximum value is <code>50</code>.</p></td><td><p>25</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>addressId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The address Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Addresses For Customer From Customer List

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:get-addresses-for-customer-from-customer-list>`

Action that returns a pageable list of all customer addresses. The default page size is 10 customer
addresses. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
`/customer/customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customer-lists/{listId}/customers/{customerNo}/addresses`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>customerNo</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Customer number.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>listId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time ## Get Assigned Product From Category

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:get-assigned-product-from-category>`

Retrieves a category product assignment. For example, if there are products A, B, and C at positions
1, 2, and 7.5, and you delete the assignment for product B, the products A, B, and C move up one
position, and position 7.5 is left null. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
`/product/catalogs/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/catalogs/{catalogId}/categories/{categoryId}/products/{productId}`
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organizationId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>catalogId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the requested catalog.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>categoryId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the category to be created.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>productId</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID (SKU) of the requested product to assign.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of this expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnectForever">ReconnectForever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Configurations

- [OAuth2 Configuration](#DataConfiguration)

### Throws

- DATA:BAD_REQUEST
- DATA:CLIENT_ERROR
- DATA:CONNECTIVITY
- DATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DATA:NOT_FOUND
- DATA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DATA:SERVER_ERROR
- DATA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DATA:TIMEOUT
- DATA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DATA:UNAUTHORIZED
- DATA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Campaign

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:get-campaign>`

Action to get campaign information. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
`/pricing/campaigns/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/campaigns/{campaignId}` endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default specific site.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>X</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use ## Get Catalog

`<commerce-cloud-dataapi:get-catalog>`

Gets the catalog with the specified information. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
`/product/catalogs/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/catalogs/{catalogId}` endpoint.

### Parameters
